item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't like it just because it spawned some "gommunism sucks no food starvation lmao 100 million dead hurr durr" enlightened americans

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Waiting patiently to see what if and when "communism will win" will say something…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The conversation log is really what makes this. Such a random yet hilarious idea put into excellent execution. +1 upvote.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Anart Antics is quite possibly my favorite series on the site. I approve this change and any other non-disruptive changes to this end.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Okay, so as was mentioned up thread, Anart Antics is a tale that features this SCP prominently, and also is a good step in facilitating the wikiwalk. An inline croslink here to there would not be so outrageous. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 appear to dislike each other and are described as debating constantly. The debates between SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 are about capitalism, communism, and the historical and current states of the United States of America, the Soviet Union, and China.

To this:

SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 appear to dislike each other and are described as debating constantly. The debates between SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 are about capitalism, communism, and the historical and current states of the United States of America, the Soviet Union, and China.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, if it instantly refills with water no matter how much it's emptied… maybe it could be used to solve world water problems? Just a theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I aware I'm a bit "late to the party" but honestly the only problem with this is it kinda lacks any ambiguity, it even kind of explains the whole scp with the "Some scientist tried this", if that was removed, and instead it was a little more vague, it add to the ambiguity, making people more curious and thus more interested in the story, otherwise its just Beauty in the Beast gone wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a huge fan of the dialogue anyway, but it's the explanation that drives this from a no-vote to a down-vote for me. It just seems so ridiculous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is great because it's so absolutely absurd and tells a very interesting story without directly telling it to the reader. Bonus points for being hilarious too. Definite +1 from me. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, since it said that draining it makes it fill at exactly the same rate, I think you just get an endless supply of water.

I DRAIN, SO THAT DEMOCRACY MAY…LIVE……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's silly… but in an endearing way and it kind of makes you wonder as to what the Hell the thought process behind that one was and also, what happened to the technology that turned these two people into a bathtub and water.

Also, presumably this tub was in a different location when it was found, so it can be moved, which begs the question: what happens if you try to knock the tub over of even turn it upside-down? Does the water still "stick" to it?

Also, the article states that subjects hear the voices when they touch the water, which made me think it was a memetic/psychic effect, but the fact that the voices could be recorded suggests otherwise. So, can everyone in the room hear the voices as long as someone is touching the water?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is weirdly cool and amusing. I usually upvote something if it evokes any kind of emotion from me and comes with some great writing.

With that being said, the writing is great and the object/interview made me smile so you get my upvote. I don't even care if doesn't make any sense, if you made me smile you deserve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really do like the SCP, but this one part just seems a bit lacking in subtlety.

I like all the ideas, but the execution could use a little bit of work. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This and the rest of the dialogue killed it for me. I just can't believe people actually talk like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel that this is borderline -J, but i like it.
Opposite of cliche really. Don't know if anyone's even thought of a talking bathtub in a serious manner before.
Or water that talks.
Or combining the two into an infinite, stereotypical argument.

The interview log is a bit poor on form however, though quite amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed Ihp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: C- and B-class personnel are all fine by me, I just don't think I appreciate this beyond just being a bathtub where one can hear two men argue about Russian/USA history. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to remain neutral on this… until I saw Class-C and Class-B in there. Sorry, but that will never be a thing for me.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We have C- and B-class personnel now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It used to be like that, but people wanted some explanation as to the origins of the bathtub, so I put it. Sorry, you're in the minority with that opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how nonsensical and completely inane this is. My one suggestion would be remove that bit about actually getting turned into the bath and water and just leave it completely unexplained

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was my biggest problem. The thing about which I was most curious was how did these people turn into a bath and water?, not reading slightly awkwardly-written, one-dimensional stereotypes arguing pointlessly.

Also, there's a few bits of awkward phrasing (for example: "Any substance added to the water inside of SCP-1974 will be drained or filtered out through unknown means. If drained, SCP-1974 will refill itself…" - the repetition of 'drained' here made me think at first that if it will refill itself only after a substance is added and then drained out), the researcher doesn't seem very realistic, and some of the formatting is a bit off.

But mostly I find it just too silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 appear to dislike each other and are described as debating constantly. The debates between SCP-1974-1 and SCP-1974-2 are about capitalism, communism, and the historical and current states of the United States of America, the Soviet Union, China.

Annnddd upvoted, I don't know why but I've always been a sucker for cold war era feuds, especially when it's two old geezers arguing about economics for all eternity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted, but personally I though that the part where he goes like:

"Remember why we're here, tubby? It's because big smart American though it would be a great idea to try to turn a Soviet POW into a tub of water. Then you thought 'hey, I wonder if I could be a bathtub?'. Well guess what? You can and now we're both trapped here, dammit!"

Was a little bit too surreal. Because like: They turned into a bathtub and water: Ok. They engage in very silly debates to pass time: Kinda makes sense.

But the notion some American scientist thought it was a good idea to turn a POW into water, and that an American agent thought it would be neat to be the bathtub containing that water, with absolutely no explanation?

That's just…silly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is well written, but still not very amusing. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is technically Series 2, but I still see what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral vote. Series 3 is infested with silliness and this isn't helping, plus the bathtub element is a little too lolrandom for me. But despite myself I found the interview log amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was the point, but fair opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't even know how many Liberty Prime jokes I heard in chat while getting this reviewed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem is that the SCP is pretty much the interview and that the doctor generally detracts from it. I'd suggest reworking the doctor's actions- he's a professional interacting with a supernatural object, even if the object acts like it's three.
Also, I found the viewpoints of both people to be a little extreme and idealistic. If that was intentional, that's fine, but I think making them a little more three-dimensional would help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DEMOCRACY IS TRUTH. COMMUNISM IS DEATH. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I share weizhong's concerns, but I smiled, so I guess it's an upvote. I would like it much more if the researcher sounded like an actual researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Be careful what you wish for, especially if your wishes are dumb and incolce being turned into a bathub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, I like it a lot. I'm just imagining the doctor probably lying in the tub and just talking to water.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was mostly good, but I still have to novote because of the researcher, like I mentioned in chat.

EDIT: Upvoted because of the edits to the researcher, but he still sounds a little off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would've helped this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was… okay, but the doctor seems a little off. I can't put my finger on it, though.
The interview is really what carries this SCP, but it fades out a little towards the end.
I'm torn on this. I could vote either way.

item.title: Author Comments
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to MissMercurial, Zolgamax, Djoric, FortuneFavorsBold, Weizhong, AndarielHalo, Bryx, and anyone I might've forgotten who reviewed this in chat.

Also thanks to Voct and Salvagebar for helping me with the concept.

Edits:

TwistedGears wrote a tale with this skip for the Under-appreciated contest in early 2014. It's called Anart Antics and y'all should all go read it because it's great.
